channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Stylish Samurai Boss Rush Game Gurei Locks In July Release Date
item.content:encoded:
Samurai boss rush game Gurei has landed a July release date, developer Lobo Sagaz and publisher Astrolabe Games have announced, so get ready to unsheath your katana and slash your way through some of the game's fearsome Kami bosses.

As revealed during today's Latin American Games Showcase, Gurei will be landing on PC via Steam on July 23rd, bringing with it "a hand-drawn art style, fluid animations, and a deep combat system built around player choice", according to Lobo Sagaz. If you're into the likes of Furi or Cuphead, this one's for you.

Gurei will allow you to tackle its myriad boss encounters in whichever order you choose, and the game will dynamically adjust its difficulty in order to accommodate your choices. I guess you can throw Titan Souls into the mix as well.

Each Kami boss you defeat will grant you a new combat ability, including "a stronger dash, a weapon that can execute enemies, [and] the ability to heal", plus lots more. You'll need to fight them yourself to discover what they grant you, of course.

While you'll be spending most of your time in Gurei battling strong bosses, you'll also be able to meet a host of Japanese mythological creatures and characters, and they'll offer "commentary, unique animations, side objectives, and obscure secrets".

They're not the only chatty ones, either; each Kami you defeat will take up residence in Rei's consciousness, and they'll be able to talk to you about what's going on, providing more unique perspectives on Gurei's idiosyncratic world.

Sometimes, Lobo Sagaz says, the Kami will provide helpful hints about the game's lore, but at other times, "they will be...not so helpful". Hope you like gods trolling you while you're trying to get some slaying done.

Gurei lands on PC via Steam on July 23rd. You can check out a demo of the game right now if you want to know whether it's for you or not.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
